pattern_recognition: Cycle Detection in Linked Lists; signals include repeated node visits during traversal.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Check each node against all other nodes to see if any node is revisited.
approaches.algorithm: Traverse the list with two nested loops; for each node, traverse the rest of the list to check for cycles.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Use two pointers moving at different speeds to detect cycles efficiently.
approaches.algorithm: Initialize two pointers, slow and fast; move slow by one step and fast by two steps; if they meet, a cycle exists.

dry_run: Consider the linked list with values [3, 2, 0, -4] and a cycle starting at position 1. Start with slow and fast pointers at the head. In the first step, slow moves to 2 and fast moves to 0. In the next step, slow moves to 0 and fast moves to 2. Finally, both pointers meet at 2, confirming a cycle.
edge_cases: An empty linked list (head is null), a single node with no cycle, a single node pointing to itself, a linked list with duplicate values, and a very large linked list to test performance.
common_pitfalls: 1) Not handling the case of an empty list correctly; 2) Confusing the cycle detection logic with simple traversal; 3) Failing to reset pointers properly when checking for cycles.
